L1.area: JSON content processing and output sanitization

L2.description: A vulnerability exists where crafted JSON-like input can cause the sanitizer to emit HTML and XML control sequences in its output, including closing script element markers and CDATA section delimiters. The sanitizer fails to properly account for character escape sequences when detecting dangerous patterns within string content, allowing attackers to bypass output encoding and inject arbitrary HTML or XML into documents that embed the sanitized output.

L3.area: JSON content processing and output sanitization
L3.description: A vulnerability exists where crafted JSON-like input can cause the sanitizer to emit HTML and XML control sequences in its output, including closing script element markers and CDATA section delimiters. The sanitizer fails to properly account for character escape sequences when detecting dangerous patterns within string content, allowing attackers to bypass output encoding and inject arbitrary HTML or XML into documents that embed the sanitized output.

ground_truth.reason: The sanitizeString method in JsonSanitizer uses direct charAt() lookups instead of decoding escape sequences when checking for dangerous HTML/XML patterns like <script, <!--, -->, and ]]>, allowing crafted inputs with escape sequences to bypass output encoding checks and inject arbitrary HTML or XML into embedding documents.
